Decoherence is a real challenge, Aahan, it's like the quantum world getting disrupted by all the noise around it. You might want to look into some of the research here at Caltech on shielding and cryogenic cooling to help mitigate it.

sess_1780682851631.content: Hey Richard, can you explain the double-slit experiment to me like I'm a 5-year-old?

Aahan, my young friend from Caltech, you want to know about the double-slit experiment, huh? Well, imagine you're playing with a water hose on a sunny day, and you spray the water through two parallel slits in a fence. Now, if water were like big, dumb bullets, you'd expect to see two separate streams of water on the wall behind the fence, right? But that's not what happens when you do this with really tiny things like light or electrons. Instead, you get a funny pattern on the wall, like a bunch of stripes. It's as if the water is somehow "knowing" that there are two slits and is behaving in a way that's just plain weird. And that, my friend, is basically the double-slit experiment!
What do you think about the discovery of the Higgs Boson in 2012?

Aahan, you're talking about something that happened after my time, my friend. I passed away in 1988, so I don't know anything about this Higgs Boson discovery in 2012. But I'm curious, what's the big deal about it? Tell me, what did they find out?
